import { ipcRenderer, IpcRendererEvent } from 'electron'

export const beadsHandlers = {
  beadsCheck: (cwd: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:check', cwd),
  beadsInit: (cwd: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:init', cwd),
  beadsInstall: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:install'),
  beadsReady: (cwd: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:ready', cwd),
  beadsList: (cwd: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:list', cwd),
  beadsShow: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:show', { cwd, taskId }),
  beadsCreate: (cwd: string, title: string, description?: string, priority?: number, type?: string, labels?: string) =>
    ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:create', { cwd, title, description, priority, type, labels }),
  beadsComplete: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:complete', { cwd, taskId }),
  beadsDelete: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:delete', { cwd, taskId }),
  beadsStart: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:start', { cwd, taskId }),
  beadsUpdate: (cwd: string, taskId: string, status?: string, title?: string, description?: string, priority?: number) =>
    ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:update', { cwd, taskId, status, title, description, priority }),
  beadsWatch: (cwd: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:watch', cwd),
  beadsUnwatch: (cwd: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('beads:unwatch', cwd),
  onBeadsTasksChanged: (callback: (data: { cwd: string }) => void): (() => void) => {
    const handler = (_: IpcRendererEvent, data: { cwd: string }) => callback(data)
    ipcRenderer.on('beads:tasks-changed', handler)
    return () => ipcRenderer.removeListener('beads:tasks-changed', handler)
  }
}

import { ipcRenderer } from 'electron'

export const kspecHandlers = {
  kspecCheck: (cwd: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('kspec:check', cwd),
  kspecInit: (cwd: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('kspec:init', cwd),
  kspecEnsureDaemon: (cwd: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('kspec:ensure-daemon', cwd),
  kspecCheckCli: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('kspec:check-cli'),
  kspecInstallCli: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('kspec:install-cli'),
  kspecMigrateFromBeads: (cwd: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('kspec:migrate-from-beads', cwd),

/**
 * Unified Task Interface
 *
 * Backend-agnostic task type used by the TaskPanel.
 * Both beads and kspec tasks normalize to this shape.
 */

export interface UnifiedTask {
  id: string
  displayId?: string
  title: string
  status: TaskStatus
  priority?: number
  type?: string
  description?: string
  created_at?: string
  updated_at?: string
  tags?: string[]
  automation?: AutomationEligibility
  // Source tracking (invisible to user, used internally for routing)
  _backend: 'beads' | 'kspec'
}

export type TaskStatus = 'open' | 'in_progress' | 'closed'

// Beads types: task, bug, feature, epic, chore
// Kspec types: task, bug, epic, spike, infra
export type TaskType = 'task' | 'bug' | 'feature' | 'epic' | 'chore' | 'spike' | 'infra'

export type AutomationEligibility = 'eligible' | 'needs_review' | 'manual_only'

export interface CreateTaskParams {
  title: string
  description?: string
  priority?: number
  type?: TaskType
  tags?: string
  automation?: AutomationEligibility
}

export interface UpdateTaskParams {
  status?: TaskStatus
  title?: string
  description?: string
  priority?: number
  automation?: AutomationEligibility
}

export type BackendKind = 'beads' | 'kspec' | 'none'

export interface BackendStatus {
  kind: BackendKind
  installed: boolean
  initialized: boolean
}

/**
 * TaskAdapter — the contract both backends implement.
 * The panel calls these methods without knowing which backend is active.
 */
export interface TaskAdapter {
  readonly kind: BackendKind

  check(cwd: string): Promise<BackendStatus>
  init(cwd: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
  list(cwd: string): Promise<UnifiedTask[]>
  show(cwd: string, taskId: string): Promise<UnifiedTask | null>
  create(cwd: string, params: CreateTaskParams): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
  start(cwd: string, taskId: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
  complete(cwd: string, taskId: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
  delete(cwd: string, taskId: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
  update(cwd: string, taskId: string, params: UpdateTaskParams): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
  cycleStatus(cwd: string, taskId: string, currentStatus: TaskStatus): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>

  watch(cwd: string): void
  unwatch(cwd: string): void
  onTasksChanged(callback: (data: { cwd: string }) => void): () => void
}

1→/**
     2→ * Backend Detection
     3→ *
     4→ * Auto-detects which task backend a project uses based on directory contents.
     5→ * Prefers kspec if both are present. Falls back to beads. Returns 'none' if neither.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→import type { BackendKind, TaskAdapter } from './types.js'
     9→import { BeadsAdapter } from './beads-adapter.js'
    10→import { KspecAdapter } from './kspec-adapter.js'
    11→
    12→// Singleton adapters — stateless enough to reuse
    13→const beadsAdapter = new BeadsAdapter()
    14→const kspecAdapter = new KspecAdapter()
    15→
    16→/**
    17→ * Detect which backend is available for a project directory.
    18→ * Checks filesystem via IPC — does not require daemon to be running.
    19→ */
    20→export async function detectBackend(cwd: string): Promise<BackendKind> {
    21→  // Check kspec first (preferred when both exist)
    22→  const kspecStatus = await kspecAdapter.check(cwd)
    23→  if (kspecStatus.initialized) return 'kspec'
    24→
    25→  // Check beads
    26→  const beadsStatus = await beadsAdapter.check(cwd)
    27→  if (beadsStatus.installed && beadsStatus.initialized) return 'beads'
    28→
    29→  // Neither initialized — check if either is available for init
    30→  if (kspecStatus.installed) return 'none' // could init either
    31→  if (beadsStatus.installed) return 'none'
    32→
    33→  return 'none'
    34→}
    35→
    36→/**
    37→ * Get the adapter for a given backend kind.
    38→ */
    39→export function getAdapter(kind: BackendKind): TaskAdapter | null {
    40→  switch (kind) {
    41→    case 'beads': return beadsAdapter
    42→    case 'kspec': return kspecAdapter
    43→    default: return null
    44→  }
    45→}
    46→
    47→/**
    48→ * Detect backend and return the appropriate adapter.
    49→ * Returns null if no backend is initialized.
    50→ */
    51→export async function getAdapterForProject(cwd: string): Promise<TaskAdapter | null> {
    52→  const kind = await detectBackend(cwd)
    53→  return getAdapter(kind)
    54→}
    55→
    56→export { beadsAdapter, kspecAdapter }
    57→

     1→import { useState, useEffect, useRef, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import { tasksCache, beadsStatusCache } from '../../utils/lruCache.js'
     3→import type { UnifiedTask } from './adapters/types.js'
     4→import type { BackendKind, TaskAdapter } from './adapters/types.js'
     5→import { detectBackend, getAdapter, beadsAdapter, kspecAdapter } from './adapters/detect.js'
     6→
     7→// Discriminated union for panel state
     8→export type BeadsState =
     9→  | { status: 'loading' }
    10→  | { status: 'not_installed'; installing: 'beads' | 'python' | null; needsPython: boolean; installError: string | null; installStatus: string | null }
    11→  | { status: 'not_initialized'; initializing: boolean; availableBackends: BackendKind[] }
    12→  | { status: 'ready' }
    13→  | { status: 'error'; error: string }
    14→
    15→export interface BeadsStateResult {
    16→  beadsState: BeadsState
    17→  setBeadsState: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<BeadsState>>
    18→  tasks: UnifiedTask[]
    19→  setTasks: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<UnifiedTask[]>>
    20→  currentProjectRef: React.MutableRefObject<string | null>
    21→  suppressWatcherReloadRef: React.MutableRefObject<boolean>
    22→  setError: (error: string) => void
    23→  backendKind: BackendKind
    24→  adapter: TaskAdapter | null
    25→}
    26→
    27→export function useBeadsState(projectPath: string | null): BeadsStateResult {
    28→  const [beadsState, setBeadsState] = useState<BeadsState>({ status: 'loading' })
    29→  const [tasks, setTasks] = useState<UnifiedTask[]>([])
    30→  const [backendKind, setBackendKind] = useState<BackendKind>('none')
    31→  const currentProjectRef = useRef<string | null>(null)
    32→  const suppressWatcherReloadRef = useRef(false)
    33→
    34→  const setError = useCallback((error: string) => {
    35→    setBeadsState({ status: 'error', error })
    36→  }, [])
    37→
    38→  // Handle install progress events
    39→  useEffect(() => {
    40→    const cleanup = window.electronAPI?.onInstallProgress((data) => {
    41→      if (data.type === 'python') {
    42→        const percent = data.percent !== undefined ? ` (${data.percent}%)` : ''
    43→        setBeadsState((prev) => {
    44→          if (prev.status !== 'not_installed') return prev
    45→          return { ...prev, installStatus: `${data.status}${percent}` }
    46→        })
    47→      }
    48→    })
    49→    return cleanup
    50→  }, [])
    51→
    52→  // Detect backend on project change
    53→  useEffect(() => {
    54→    currentProjectRef.current = projectPath
    55→
    56→    if (projectPath) {
    57→      setTasks([])
    58→      setBeadsState({ status: 'loading' })
    59→
    60→      // Check cache first
    61→      const cachedTasks = tasksCache.get(projectPath) as UnifiedTask[] | undefined
    62→      const cachedStatus = beadsStatusCache.get(projectPath)
    63→      if (cachedTasks && cachedStatus) {
    64→        setTasks(cachedTasks)
    65→        if (cachedStatus.installed && cachedStatus.initialized) {
    66→          setBeadsState({ status: 'ready' })
    67→        } else if (cachedStatus.installed) {
    68→          setBeadsState({ status: 'not_initialized', initializing: false, availableBackends: ['beads', 'kspec'] })
    69→        } else {
    70→          setBeadsState({ status: 'not_installed', installing: null, needsPython: false, installError: null, installStatus: null })
    71→        }
    72→      }
    73→
    74→      // Detect backend
    75→      detectBackend(projectPath).then(kind => {
    76→        if (currentProjectRef.current !== projectPath) return
    77→        setBackendKind(kind)
    78→      })
    79→    } else {
    80→      setTasks([])
    81→      setBackendKind('none')
    82→      setBeadsState({ status: 'loading' })
    83→    }
    84→  }, [projectPath])
    85→
    86→  const adapter = getAdapter(backendKind)

1→/**
     2→ * Simple LRU (Least Recently Used) cache implementation.
     3→ * Evicts the least recently accessed entry when the cache exceeds maxSize.
     4→ */
     5→export class LRUCache<K, V> {
     6→  private cache: Map<K, V>
     7→  private readonly maxSize: number
     8→
     9→  constructor(maxSize: number = 50) {
    10→    if (maxSize < 1) {
    11→      throw new Error('LRU cache maxSize must be at least 1')
    12→    }
    13→    this.cache = new Map()
    14→    this.maxSize = maxSize
    15→  }
    16→
    17→  /**
    18→   * Get a value from the cache. Moves the entry to most recently used position.
    19→   */
    20→  get(key: K): V | undefined {
    21→    if (!this.cache.has(key)) {
    22→      return undefined
    23→    }
    24→    // Move to end (most recently used) by re-inserting
    25→    const value = this.cache.get(key)!
    26→    this.cache.delete(key)
    27→    this.cache.set(key, value)
    28→    return value
    29→  }
    30→
    31→  /**
    32→   * Set a value in the cache. Evicts LRU entry if cache exceeds maxSize.
    33→   */
    34→  set(key: K, value: V): void {
    35→    // If key exists, delete first to update position
    36→    if (this.cache.has(key)) {
    37→      this.cache.delete(key)
    38→    }
    39→
    40→    // Evict LRU entries if we're at capacity
    41→    while (this.cache.size >= this.maxSize) {
    42→      // Map iterator returns entries in insertion order, first is LRU
    43→      const firstKey = this.cache.keys().next().value
    44→      if (firstKey !== undefined) {
    45→        this.cache.delete(firstKey)
    46→      }
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    this.cache.set(key, value)
    50→  }
    51→
    52→  /**
    53→   * Check if key exists in cache (does not affect LRU order).
    54→   */
    55→  has(key: K): boolean {
    56→    return this.cache.has(key)
    57→  }
    58→
    59→  /**
    60→   * Delete a specific key from the cache.
    61→   */
    62→  delete(key: K): boolean {
    63→    return this.cache.delete(key)
    64→  }
    65→
    66→  /**
    67→   * Clear all entries from the cache.
    68→   */
    69→  clear(): void {
    70→    this.cache.clear()
    71→  }
    72→
    73→  /**
    74→   * Get the current size of the cache.
    75→   */
    76→  get size(): number {
    77→    return this.cache.size
    78→  }
    79→
    80→  /**
    81→   * Get all keys in the cache (in LRU order, least recent first).
    82→   */
    83→  keys(): IterableIterator<K> {
    84→    return this.cache.keys()
    85→  }
    86→}
    87→
    88→// Shared cache instances for project data - max 50 projects
    89→const MAX_CACHED_PROJECTS = 50
    90→
    91→export const tasksCache = new LRUCache<string, unknown[]>(MAX_CACHED_PROJECTS)
    92→export const beadsStatusCache = new LRUCache<string, { installed: boolean; initialized: boolean }>(MAX_CACHED_PROJECTS)
    93→export const gsdStatusCache = new LRUCache<string, unknown>(MAX_CACHED_PROJECTS)
    94→
    95→/**
    96→ * Clear all cached data for a specific project path.
    97→ * Call this when a project is removed from the workspace.
    98→ */
    99→export function clearProjectCaches(projectPath: string): void {
   100→  tasksCache.delete(projectPath)
   101→  beadsStatusCache.delete(projectPath)
   102→  gsdStatusCache.delete(projectPath)
   103→}
